work: Indo-European Cognate Relationships database (IE-CoR release v1.2): Hittite core vocabulary
citation: Heggarty, Paul & Anderson, Cormac & Scarborough, Matthew 2024. Indo-European Cognate Relationships database (IE-CoR version 1.1). Leipzig: Max Planck Institute for Evolutionary Anthropology
conversion: 133 Hittite forms across 130 IE-CoR core concepts extracted from the v1.2 CLDF release by scripts/import-iecor.mjs; 4 explicitly reconstructed headwords excluded, 5 reconstructed syllabic spellings omitted, and 8 reconstruction-bearing source notes replaced with an omission notice under the app's attested-form policy; authoritative English concept names used as display and meaning-search guide senses, while differing contributor-supplied gloss wording is retained separately as possibly non-English, non-indexed source data; source form IDs, Concepticon links, attested transliterated spellings, other scholarly notes and cognate-set IDs retained. This is a core-vocabulary wordlist, not a comprehensive Hittite dictionary.

entries.note: This IE-CoR note discusses a reconstructed comparative form; the form and note are omitted under Ancient Lexicon's attested-form policy.
Cf. HW² I 187-191 s.v. (UZU)iškiš- 'Rücken, Rückhalt' for attestations and further discussion.
Cf. HW² I 293-302 s.v. idalu- 'böse, böswillig, ungünstig, schlecht, schlimm' for attestations and discussion.
Native script form šar-ḫu-u-u̯a-an-da-an is acc.sg.; the nom.sg. form is not attested, cf. CHD Š 253-254 for attestations and discussion.
Cf. CHD Š 92-100 for attestations and discussion.

Form attested only once (nom.pl. wa-at-ta-e-eš). Identification with Sum. MUŠEN (Friedrich 1927:190 1) is generally accepted. The word (a diphthong stem) is probably inherited. We have added this term as the best currently-known potential candidate, while acknowledging that due to the fragmentary attestation of Hittite a more suitable target lexeme may have existed, but is unattested. The CPHL dataset included for this concept 'suwais (?)' regarding which Kloekhorst (EDHIL 795) accepts the analysis of Y. Cohen (2010. Rara avis: A study of the HU section of the Sa vocabulary. In H. Baker, E. Robson, & G. Zólyomi eds., Your praise is sweet. A memorial volume for Jeremy Black from students, colleagues and friends. London pp. 29-40. British Institute for the Study of Iraq.) that this word does not mean 'bird', but rather 'rejection'.

Old ablauting ḫi-verb (wāk-/wakk-, typ āk-/akk- ‘to die’). Cf. tentatively Pal. wakk- ‘id.?’, too. For the etymology and semantic interpretation, cf. EDHIL 939-940; cf. Oettinger 1979: 444-445 for attestations.
Cf. EDHIL 829 s.v. dankui- / dankuu̯ai- for a survey of the forms attested and further discussion.
heteroclitic noun ēšḫar/išḫan-, cf. HW² E 115-121 s.v. ešḫar (išḫar) 'Blut' for attestations and further discussion.

entries.sourceGloss: bone, (fig.) strength, a measure of length
entries.note: diphthong-stem inflection. cf. within Anatolian CLuw. ḫāš- ‘bone’ and HLuw. has- ‘force(?)’ (abl.-instr. “no. 314”ha-sa-ti-i ‘by force’ KARKAMIŠ A11c §30). Cf. HW² Ḫ 425- s.v. ḫaštāi- 'Knochen' for attestations and further discussion.
This IE-CoR note discusses a reconstructed comparative form; the form and note are omitted under Ancient Lexicon's attested-form policy.
Cf. HW² A 60 s.v. alpa- 'Wolke' for attestations and interpretation.
alternation ekuna/ikuna-. Cf. HW² E 33 s.v. ekuna- (ikuna-) 'kalt' for attestations and interpretation.
Cf. EDHIL 992-993 s.v. u̯e-/uu̯a- 'to come' for a survey of the forms attested and further discussion.
Cf. HED K 66-71 s.v. kappuwai- 'tally, count, reckon, calculate' for attestations and discussion. Lexeme citation form follows EDHIL 440.
Cf. CHD Š 494-504 s.v. šiwatt- 'day' for citations and further discussion.

entries.sourceGloss: to die, to be killed, to be eclipsed
entries.note: Cf. HED A+E/I 17-23 s.v. ak(k)- / ek- 'die, be killed' for attestations.
Cf. s.v. CHD P 235-237 s.v. padda- 'to dig (the ground)' etc. for attestations and discussion.
Cf. iškii̯e/a- 'to smear, to daub, to salve', cf. Tischler TEG A-K p.404.
Cf. HW² E 29-33 s.v. eku- / aku- 'trinken' for attestations and discussion.
Cf. HW² Ḫ 478-482 s.v. ḫat- "aus/vertrocknen" with ptcp ḫatant- "trocken, getrocknet" for attestations and discussion.
Cf. HW² I 234-236 s.v. (UZU)ištamana-, ištamin(a)- 'Ohr' for attestations and further discussion.
Cf. EDHIL 858-862 s.v. tēkan / takn- for a survey of forms attested and further discussion.
Cf. HW² E 128-138 s.v. ed-/ad- 'essen, fressen' for attestations and discussion.
Nom.sg. is only attested through Sumerogram IGI or Akkadogram ĪNU. The given native script form ša-a-ku-u̯a is nom.pl., cf. CHD Š 65-77 for attestations and further discussion.
Cf. CHD L-N 211-213 for attestations and further discussion.
adv., cf. EDHIL 904-905 for a survey of forms attested and further discussion.
Cf. CHD L-N 338-341 for attestations and further discussion.
(r/n-stem) pattar/pattan- 'wing, feather', see also partāu̯ar/partaun- 'wing, feather'. Cf. CHD P 240-241 for attestations and further discussion.

entries.sourceGloss: to smash, to defeat, to strike/beat (in conflict)
entries.note: Cf. HW² Ḫ 685-689 s.v. ḫulla/e- trans. und Med. '(nieders)schlagen (milit.), (obj.) zerschlagen, anfechten, bestreiten, verwerfen (jur.)', corresponds in meaning to '(im Kampf) schlagen' Akkad. abāku bzw. dawdâ dâku.

entries.sourceGloss: nail, a unit of linear measure
entries.note: Cf. CHD Š 180-181 for attestations and discussion.
Cf. CHD P 12-16 for attestations and further discussion.
Cf. HW² A 58-59 for attestations and discussion.

The spelling (and etymological interpretation) of this word follows EDHIL 655-656 where Kloekhorst convincingly argues that the first syllable should be read pat. For the target sense see CHD P 354-355 s.v. piddai-, pittiya-, pitte-, 4.
Cf. EDHIL 971-972 s.v. u̯arša- 'fog, mist' for a survey of forms attested and discussion.

The lexeme is normally spelled using the Sumerogram GÌR (Akk. ŠĒPU), e.g. nom.sg. GÌR-aš. The native script form given, pa-a-tu-u[š] is acc.pl. Also attested are instr. pa-te-et, gen.pl. pa-ta-a-an, and dat.-loc.pl. pa-ta-a-aš. According to Kloekhorst (2008:653-654) the OH forms may reflect the original athematic root noun, while the thematic pata- forms attested in NH are secondary innovations. Cf. CHD P 231-235 for attestations and further discussion.
perhaps derived from the adjective 'little': 'little hand', the fingers minus the thumb (Gvozdanovic 1992:77). Cf. CHD L-N 308 for attestations.
Form i-ga-it 3.sg.pret.act. (3.sg.pres.act. not attested). Cf. HW² E 28 s.v. egai- / igai- 'erkalten' for attestations and discussion. Perhaps not the precise target lexeme.
Cf. CHD Š 445-448 s.v. šēša-, šešan- 'fruit; fruit tree' for attestations and discussion.
Cf. CHD P 657-660 s.v. šūu-, šū- 'full' for attestations and discussion.

entries.sourceGloss: to give, to pay, to grant, to hand over
entries.note: Cf. CHD P 40-55 for attestations and discussion.
Cf. HW² A 492-527 s.v. āššu- 'gut' for attestations and discussion.
Cf. EDHIL 971-972 s.v. u̯ēlku- 'grass, vegetation' for a survey of forms attested and discussion.
Cf. HW² Ḫ 12 s.v. ḫaḫli(u)u̯ant- 'gelb (grün)' for attestations and interpretation.
Native script form te-e-da-an is acc.sg. (cf. Tischler HEG T p.345).
Cf. HW² K 313-326 s.v. (UZU)keššar-, (UZU)kišši/ara- 'Hand' for attestations and discussion.
Cf. HW² Ḫ 344-357 s-v- ḫaršar 'Kopf' for attestations and discussion.
Cf. HW² I 236-242 s.v. ištamaš- 'hören' for attestations and discussion.
Cf. HED K 189-191 s.v. kir(ti), kart(i) 'heart; center, core' and EDHIL 469-471 s.v. ker / kard(i) 'heart; center, core' for a survey of forms attested and discussion.
'strong, powerful, heavy', Cf. Tischler HEG T 259-260.
Cf. EDHIL 945-946 s.v. u̯alḫ- 'to hit, to strike' for a survey of forms attested and discussion.
Oblique ka-ra-ú-ni is dat.-loc.sg. Cf. HW² K 139-144 s.v. karawar 'Gehörn, Horn (von Tieren)' for attestations and discussion.

Native script form ḫu-ur-nu-u̯a-an-zi is an infinitive. The 3.sg.pres. is only attested as an imperfective stem [ḫ]u-u-u̯a-ar-ni-iš-ke-ez-zi. Cf. HW² K 761 s.v. ḫurna-, ḫuwarna- 'jagen, (Gebiet) bejagen' for attestations and discussion
Cf. HW² E 27-28 s.v. eka- 'Eis' for attestations and discussion
Cf. EDHIL 485-486 s.v. kue(n)- / kun- /kuu̯a(n)- 'to kill, to slay, to ruin' for a survey of forms attested and discussion.
Cf. HW² K 293-298 s.v. (UZU) genu-, (UZU) ginu- 'Knie' for attestations and discussion

entries.sourceGloss: to know, to experience, to pay attention to, to heed
entries.note: Cf. CHD Š 21-32 for attestations and discussion.
Cf. CHD L-N 80-82 for attestations and discussion.

Onomatopoeic verb, attested once in KUB XXXIII 120 (+119 + XXXVI 31) I 26f.: man ᵈKumarbiš ŠA ᵈAnu LÚ-natar katta pašta (27) n=aš=za duškatta n=aš=za ḫa-aḫ-ḫar-aš-ta "When Kumarbi gobbled down Anu's manhood, he rejoiced and laughed." (cf. HW² Ḫ).
Cf. CHD P 190-191 s.v. (GIŠ)paršdu- 'leaf, foliage' for attestations and discussion.
Lexeme refers to a 'fleshy part of the leg' (HW² E). Further semantics and etymology are unclear.
Cf. HW² Ḫ 654-655 s.v. ḫuiš- ḫueš-, (ḫuš-) 'leben, am Leben sein; am Leben bleiben' for attestations and discussion
Cf. CHD L-N 72 for attestations and discussion.
This IE-CoR note discusses a reconstructed comparative form; the form and note are omitted under Ancient Lexicon's attested-form policy.
Nom.sg. always uses Sumerographic writing LÚ-aš. Native script form reconstructed based on attested gen.sg. pé-eš-na-aš.
Cf. HW² A 48-49  s.v. aiš- / išš- 'Mund' for attestations and discussion
Cf. CHD L-N 31-36 for attestations and discussion
Cf. HW² Ḫ 513 s.v. ḫatku- 'eng, hart schwierig' for attestations and discussion.
This IE-CoR note discusses a reconstructed comparative form; the form and note are omitted under Ancient Lexicon's attested-form policy.
This IE-CoR note discusses a reconstructed comparative form; the form and note are omitted under Ancient Lexicon's attested-form policy.
Cf. HW² I 207-210 s.v. išpant- 'Nacht' for attestations and discussion.
Native script form ti-it-ti-te-et is abl.-instr., cf. Tischler TEG T p.393.

Nom.sg. always spelled as 1-iš (OS), 1-aš (NS).  The native script form as given is reconstructed from gen.sg. ši-i-e-el, dat.-loc.sg. ši-e-da-ni, etc. Cf. CHD P 333-337 for full range of forms attested.

entries.sourceGloss: to be happy, to entertain (oneself), to play
entries.note: Cf. EDHIL 901-902 s.v. tušk(ii̯e/a)- 'to be happy, to entertain (oneself), to play' for a survey of attested forms and further discussion.
Cf. HW² Ḫ 672-681 s.v. ḫuittiya-, ḫuettiya-, ḫuet- 'ziehen' for attestations and discussion.

entries.sourceGloss: to push (away), to shove, to cast off
entries.note: Native script šu-(u-)u̯a-an-zi is 3.pl.pres.act.; the 3.sg.pres.act. form is unattested. Cf. EDHIL 797-798 for a survey of forms attested and discussion.
Cf. HW² Ḫ 577-579 s.v. ḫeu- 'Regen' for attestations and discussion.
We have included Hittite mit(t)a- / miti- as the best possible candidate for this meaning. For attestations and interpretation of the forms cf. CHD L-N 301-304.
Cf. EDHIL 493-494 s.v. kunna- 'right (hand or side); right, favourable, successful' for a survey of attestations and discussion.
Nom.sg. only as Sumerographic ÍD-aš; native script form reconstructed from gen.sg. ḫa-pa-aš. Cf. HW² Ḫ 197-205 s.v. ḫapa- 'Fluß, (Bach)' for attestations and discussion.
Native script form šu-ur-ki-in is acc.sg.; the nom.sg. form is unattested. Cf. CHD P 651-652 for attestations and discussion.
This IE-CoR note discusses a reconstructed comparative form; the form and note are omitted under Ancient Lexicon's attested-form policy.

entries.sourceGloss: to speak, recite, read aloud, tell, report, reveal
entries.note: Native script me-e-ma-i (KUB 31.143 ii 5, 12, 19, 25, 33, OS). See CHD L-N: 254-263 for further attestations.
Cf. HW² A 350-354 s.v. aruna- 'Meer' for attestations and discussion.
Cf. HW² A 572-627 s.v. au-/u- 'sehen' and EDHIL 227-229 s.v. au-/u- 'to see, to look' for attested forms and discussion.

The precise semantics of this lexeme remain under discussion, but it seems to be the best candidate. Cf. CHD P 257-259 s.v. šariya- B 'to embroider(?), sew on(?), decorate (cloth) with attached jewels(?); to truss(?)/sew up(?), EDHIL 727 s.v. šar-, šarii̯e/a- ‘to embroider(?), to sew on(?); to truss(?) / sew(?) up’ for attestations and discussion.

This lexeme appears as a pair with dampu- and together mean 'blunt' and 'pointed'; derivative vocabulary (alpuemar 'point, tip' and alpuēšš- vb. 'to be sharp, to be acute') suggest that dampu- means 'blunt', and therefore alpu- the counterpart meaning 'pointed'. Cf. HW² A 61 s.v. alpu- 'spitzig'(?), EDHIL 170-171 s.v. alpu- 'pointed' for attestations and further discussion with references to further literature.
This IE-CoR note discusses a reconstructed comparative form; the form and note are omitted under Ancient Lexicon's attested-form policy.
Cf. HW² I 121-128 s.v. išḫamai- 'singen, besingen' etc. for attestations and discussion.

entries.sourceGloss: ‘(midd.) to sit down, to seat oneself; (+ =ššan) to sit; (act.) to sit, to reside; (trans.) to settle
entries.note: Cf. HW² E 97-133 s.v. eš- Med.m eš-/aš- Akt. 'sitzen, sich setzen' for attestations and discussion.
Cf. CHD L-N 448-453 for attestations and discussion.

entries.sourceGloss: to sleep, to rest, to lie down
entries.note: Cf. CHD Š 439-445 for attestations and discussion.
Cf. CHD Š s.v. šup(p)-, šuppiye-, šuppa- 'to (go to) sleep, fall asleep' for attestations and discussion, From (OH/MS).
Cf. HW² K 103-107 s.v. kappi- 'klein, jung' for attestations and discussion.
Cf. s.v. EDHIL 895 s.v. tuḫḫuu̯ai- / tuḫḫui- 'smoke' for a survey of attested forms and discussion.
Cf. CHD L-N 306-308 s.v. (:)miu- 'soft, smooth, mild, gentle, pleasant, agreeable' for attestations and discussion.

different stems: illui̯anka- illii̯anka- ellii̯anku-. Determinative MUŠ (cf. also CLuv. MUŠ 'snake'). Used especially of the serpent-adversary of the Stormgod. Cf. HW² I 46-48 s.v. (MUŠ)illuyanka/u-, (MUŠ) elluyanka/u- 'Schlangenungeheuer, Schlangendrache, eine Art Schlange(?)' and HED A/E+I 358-359 s.v. illuyanka-, elliyanku- 'snake, serpent' for attestations and discussion.

entries.sourceGloss: to stand (by), to be stationed, to remain standing; to be present, to occur
entries.note: Cf. HW² A 194-219 s.v. ar- Med. intr. 'stehen, dastehen' for attestations and discussion.
Cf. HW² Ḫ 434-438 s.v. ḫašter(a)- 'Stern' for attestations and discussion.

Occurs in a ritual fragment KBo 12, 123:9f.: p]iran GIŠ.MAR.GÍD.DA unuwanda (10) GIŠ ḫu-]u-u̯a-ḫur-pa-al KÙ.BABBAR GAR.RA A-NA GU₄.ḪI.A=ya "Ahead are decked-out wagons. The stick is decorated with silver and for the oxen' (cf. HW² Ḫ 645 s.v. GIŠ ḫu(wa)ḫurpal- 'Stock'). Cf. also turi- 'spear, lance', CLuv. tura/i- 'stick, weapon'.

nom.-acc.sg.n. pé-e-ru; nom.sg.c. pé-e-ru-na-aš. Cf. CHD P 314-315 for attestations. We have included this term as the best currently-known potential candidate, while acknowledging that due to the fragmentary attestation of Hittite a more suitable target lexeme may have existed, but is unattested.
Normally written with Sumerogram ᵈUTU (± phonetic complements). Cf. HED A/E+I 465-468 for attestations and discussion.

entries.sourceGloss: to swell (said of fermented dough and of a tumor or boil on the head) (CHD P, EDHIL)
entries.note: Cf. CHD P s.v. putkiya- 'to swell (said of fermented dough and of a tumor or boil on the head)' for attestations and discussion.

Only attested in two contexts, as a body part of bears, šāša-animals, and lions, and leopards. This may mean tail, but could be some other body part shared in common to these animals. The identification of this lexeme as TAIL is therefore not entirely certain. Cf. discussion in EDHIL, CHD P 449.
This IE-CoR note discusses a reconstructed comparative form; the form and note are omitted under Ancient Lexicon's attested-form policy.
Cf. CHD P 316-323 s.v. peš(š)iya/e-, piš(š)a/e-, peššiyai-, pišya- 'to throw, cast, shove', etc. for attestations and discussion.

entries.sourceGloss: to bind, to wrap; to obligate with, to impose upon
entries.note: Cf. HW² I 113-117 s.v. išḫai-/išḫiya- 'anbinden, binden; (ein/um)wickeln; fesseln' etc. for attestations and discussion.
Cf. CHD L-N 21-25 for attestations and discussion.
Cf. HW² K 8-12 s.v. gaga-, UZU qaqa- 'Zahn' for attestations and discussion.
Frequently also spelled with Sumerogram GIŠ. Cf. EDHIL 849-850 s.v. tāru- 'wood' for a survey of attested forms and further discussion.

entries.sourceGloss: to turn, to turn someone, to turn oneself, to send
entries.note: Cf. CHD L-N 347-364 for attestations and discussion.

entries.sourceGloss: for the second time, again, subordinately
entries.note: Adverbial form. As an adjective the numeral is otherwise only attested using the logographic spelling '2' taking case endings from the pronominal declension, cf. Hoffner & Melchert (2008:156). Cf. EDHIL 826-827 s.v. tān 'for the second time, again, subordinately' for examples of forms attested and discussion.
Lit. a participle derived from the verb ā(i)- 'to be hot', cf. HW² A 44-45 s.v. a- (1) 'warm, heiß sein', EDHIL 164-165 s.v. ā(i)- 'to be hot' for attestations and discussion.
Cf. HW² A 224-234 s.v. arra- (a-)ar-r- 'waschen' for attestations and discussion.
Cf. EDHIL 987-988 s.v. u̯ātar / u̯itēn- 'water¨ for a survey of attested forms and further discussion.
Cf. HW² Ḫ 307-314 s.v. ḫarki- 'weiß' (Sumerogr. BABBAR) for attestations and discussion.
Cf. CHD P 65-67 s.v. palḫi- 'wide, broad' for attestations and discussion.
Cf. HW² Ḫ 812-815 s.v. ḫuwant- 'Wind' (Sumerogr. IM) for attestations and discussion.
Native script oblique stem form pár-ta-u-na-az is abl.; the gen.sg. is unattested. Cf. CHD P 198-199 for attestations and discussion.
Oblique stem attested via dat.-loc.pl. pád-da-na-aš and pát-ta-na-aš. Cf. CHD P 240-241 for attestations and discussion.

The oblique stem ti-e-eš-ni is dat.-loc.sg. The contexts of this word are uncertain, but the consistent use of the determinative GIŠ indicates the word has something to do with wood. It is argued that the word may be connected with the Sumerogram (GIŠ)TIR 'forest' that is sometimes phonetically complemented with a -šar/-šn- flexional ending, and so the translation 'forest' has commonly been adopted for this lexeme, although the evidence for this identification is admittedly circumstantial. We have included this word as the best possible candidate, but given the fragmentary attestation of Hittite the possibility remains open that it might not be the target lexeme.

Nom.sg. only as Sumerographic MU.KAM-za. Stem attested in syllabic spellings of oblique case forms ú-i-it-ti (dat.-loc.sg.), ú-i-[-it-ta-an], ú-it-ta-an, ú-i-it-ta-aš (gen.pl.) and derived vocab., e.g. u̯ettandātar 'period of a year', etc. Cf. EDHIL 1014-1015 s.v. u̯itt- 'year¨ for a survey of attested forms and further discussion.
Cf. HW² Ḫ 12 s.v. ḫaḫli(u)u̯ant- 'gelb (grün)' for attestations and interpretation.

Nom.sg. is always spelled as a Sumerogram É-er, (gen.sg. É-aš). The nominative stem per- is certain from dat.-loc.sg. pé-e-ri (beside pár-ni) and abl. É-er-za beside pár-na-za. Cf. CHD P 	273-291 for attestations and discussion.

derivative to militt-/malitt- 'honey'. nom.sg.com. [mi-l]i-id-du-uš, cf. nom.-acc.sg.neut. mi-li-id-du, ma-li-id-du. Cf. CHD L-N 252-253 s.v. miliddu-, maliddu-, meliddu- 'sweet (of figs, honey); pleasant (of mood or disposition [ZI] of a god)' for attestations and discussion.
Cf. CHD P 18-40 for attestations and discussion.

entries.sourceGloss: to run, to hurry, to spread (of vegetation)
entries.note: Cf. HW² Ḫ 796-806 s.v. ḫuwai- 'gehen,, ansteuern, entfliehen, entkommen, entlaufen' etc. for attestations and discussion.

entries.sourceGloss: to take (somewhere), to carry, to transport; to spend (time)
entries.note: prefix pe- and verb dā- 'to take, to wed, to decide', cf. uda- ‘to bring (here)’ from prefix u- and dā- 'id.'. Cf. CHD P 345ff. for attestations and discussion.

entries.sourceGloss: to take, to wed, to decide
entries.note: Cf. EDHIL 803-805 s.v. dā- / d- 'to take, to wed, to decide' for a survey of attested forms and further discussion.
Probably a nasal-infixed verb that was transferred to the hatrai-class, cf. CHD L-N 329-332 for attestations and discussion.
Secondarily derived from Hitt. išḫaḫru- 'tear', cf. HW² I 111-112 s.v. išḫaḫruwa(i)- 'weinen'  for attestations and discussion. Lexeme citation form follows EDHIL 391.
Cf. HW² I 6-18 s.v. iye- / iya-'machen, tun', etc. for attestations and discussion.
Cf. s.v. CHD L-N 125-126 for attestations and discussion.
